text: You walked to the square and found the cafe on the corner.
Now you walk into a store. You want to buy a gift.
Excuse me, how much is this scarf?
It is twenty dollars. But today there is a discount, so it is only fifteen.
That is cheap! And how much are these postcards?
The postcards are one dollar each. Would you like to pay with cash or card?
With cash, please. Here is twenty dollars.
Thank you. Here is your change and your receipt. Have a nice day!
I will pay with cash, please.
Can I have my change and receipt?

Welcome to the gift store. We are happy to help you find a present. Today we have a special discount on all the scarves. This blue scarf is twenty dollars, but with the discount it is only fifteen dollars. The bags near the window are more expensive; they are forty dollars each. If you want something cheap, the postcards on the table are one dollar. You can pay with cash or card at the front desk. We also give you the change and a receipt. If you buy two items, we give you a small gift. Thank you for visiting our store, and have a wonderful day.

Hi Marlene, this is Sofia. I am at the market near the square. There is a great sale here today. The dresses are not expensive; they are very cheap with the discount. I am looking at a beautiful red dress. I think it is the right size for you. If you want, come and try it on in the fitting room. Remember to bring some cash, because not every shop takes a card. I have my wallet with me, so I can help you pay. Call me when you arrive. See you soon.

First, you walk into the store and look at the items. Then you find a scarf you like and ask, how much is this? After that, the clerk tells you the price and the discount. Next, you try it on in the fitting room to check the size. Finally, you pay with cash, take your change, and keep the receipt.
The fitting room is over there.
I will pay with cash, please.
Can I have my change and receipt?
